'''Vitor Reige''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|human]] became the first [[Imperial Election of 44 ABY|democratically-elected]] [[Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|Head of State]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]] and had served as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], and had been a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] for the [[Jagged Fel]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. Reige become Head of State after Fel dropped out of a three-way contest with [[Natasi Daala]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|This is Admiral Vitor Reige, Admiral Daala. I am commander of the Bloodfin and of this Imperial strike force, led by true Head of State, Jagged Fel.|Vitor Reige to [[Natasi Daala]]|<ref name="Ascension">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''</ref>}}
During the [[Battle of Bastion]], which occurred during the latter half of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], it was Vitor who flew the shuttle that bore the grievously injured [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] from [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] to [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]]. He accompanied the Grand Admiral aboard the [[Widowmaker (frigate)|''Widowmaker'']], and refused to have his own wounds tended to until the Grand Admiral was stabilized by the [[Jedi healer/Legends|Jedi healer]], [[Tekli]].<ref name="Remnant" />

Reige was Pellaeon's top aide at the [[Second Battle of Fondor]] in [[41 ABY]] and had been serving him faithfully for years. When Pellaeon was murdered by [[Tahiri Veila]], Reige led a mutiny against the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] who had taken over Pellaeon's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Bloodfin (Star Destroyer)|''Bloodfin'']], holding out long enough to be rescued by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] forces.<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>

In [[44 ABY]] Reige, now an Admiral, commanded the ''Bloodfin'' against [[Natasi Daala]] during the [[Battle over Exodo II]].<ref name="Ascension" />

When Daala, with the help of [[Abeloth]] posing as [[Lydea Pagorski]], shortly later managed to make Fel agree to a [[Imperial Election of 44 ABY|democratic election]] for the post of Imperial [[Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|Head of State]], Fel convinced Reige to enter the contest as a spoiler candidate to split the military vote. After Fel ordered a strike on Daala's campaign headquarter - the same laboratory in which [[Tol Getelles]] developed his youth serum from [[Droch/Legends|drochs]] and the scientists who developed the [[nanovirus]] that prevented [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] from returning to [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]] - public opinion rose against Fel but he anticipated Daala's attempt to discredit him for his actions and instead chose to drop out of the race and endorsed Reige, who beat Daala easily. Reige then gave the former Head of State the yacht ''[[Parting Gift]]'' as a farewell present.<ref name="Apocalypse">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Apocalypse]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]] novel, ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'', it's implied that Pellaeon may have fathered Vitor Reige with an unidentified woman, though Pellaeon never confirmed Reige's ancestry.<ref name="Revelation" /> This would be supported in the [[Star Wars: Fate of the Jedi|''Fate of the Jedi'']] novel ''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]'', when Admiral Daala believes that Reige sounds like a younger Pellaeon.
